doc:
instagram — the Spock authority in the canonical full-stack framework example.

RFD 0024 IMPLEMENTATION PREVIEW — EXPERIMENTAL, UNSTABLE, AND
NON-NORMATIVE. The `0.5.2` toolchain accepts the top-level `error`
declarations in this file as implementation evidence, not accepted v0 or a
compatibility promise.

This program is the live authority behind the sibling Uhura client. Its
tables and seed mirror the demo's
standard fixture (fixtures/standard.toml), with the fixture's generated
images materialized as Spock storage objects. Its fns satisfy the feed,
comments, profile, people, story, saved-library, and create port contracts.
The client-owned Play provider assembles projection shapes from Spock GraphQL,
signs storage downloads, and maps port commands onto `POST /rest/v1/rpc/*`.

Identity is the v0 dev seam (RFD 0014): the driver acts as Mira by sending
`X-Spock-Actor: 10000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001` — user keys are
literal uuids in the seed, so ids are stable across restarts.

Image and video file columns are `storage_object` references. Existing demo
JPEGs and MP4s enter through `file("./seed/…")`; uploaded bytes enter through
Spock's signed storage protocol and can be attached with `create_image_post`.

Counts are intentionally absent from the stored model. Posts, likes,
comments, follows, saves, and story views are source rows; the Play provider
derives every number and label shown by Uhura from a GraphQL snapshot. Saves
remain private viewer state and are never presented as public engagement.
The 162-row demo seed exercises 9 actors, 23 posts, 12 story frames, and the
three stored-media shapes: image, carousel, and playable video plus poster.

errors.doc: The current actor is absent or is not permitted to perform the operation.
The uploaded image is not ready to be attached to a post.
The uploaded object is not a supported image type.

tables.name: user
tables.doc:
A demo person. The identity anchor: `user.id` is what `X-Spock-Actor`
carries and `spock_actor()` returns. Profile counts are derived from post
and follow rows instead of being stored here as decorative labels.

fields.name: avatar
fields.doc: The avatar image stored in Spock's byte plane.

tables.name: follow
tables.doc:
A directed follower edge. Profile follower/following counts and people
lists are computed from these rows.

tables.name: story
tables.doc:
One active story frame. Authors can own a sequence of frames; position is
unique within that active sequence so playback order is deterministic.

fields.name: position
fields.doc: Position within the author's current story, 1-based.

fields.name: media_file
fields.doc: Story image stored in Spock's byte plane.

fields.name: published_at
fields.doc: Real publication instant; the provider formats relative time.

tables.name: story_view
tables.doc:
A viewer has seen a specific story frame. Ring state is derived: an
author's ring is unseen when at least one current frame lacks this edge.

tables.name: post
tables.doc:
A published post. `media_kind` selects the port's media union variant: an
`image` row carries `media_file`/`media_alt`; a `video` row additionally
carries playable `video_file` bytes while retaining `media_file` as its
poster; a `carousel` row leaves those fields absent and owns
`carousel_slide` rows instead.

fields.name: caption
fields.doc: Caption text. An empty string means the author published without one.

fields.name: published_at
fields.doc: Real publication instant; the provider derives age labels and ordering.

fields.name: show_in_feed
fields.doc:
Whether this post participates in the home feed. Older profile posts
remain first-class posts but do not flood the short demo feed.

fields.name: media_kind
fields.doc: Which media union variant this post renders as.

fields.name: media_file
fields.doc: Image or video-poster object; absent for carousels.

fields.name: video_file
fields.doc: Playable video bytes; present only for video posts.

fields.name: media_alt
fields.doc: Alt text for `media_file`; absent for carousels.

tables.name: post_tag
tables.doc:
A person tagged in a published post. The composite key prevents duplicate
tags while keeping the post itself as the single media/caption authority
used by feed, profile-grid, tagged-grid, and post-detail projections. A tag
disappears with either endpoint: deleting a post cannot leave a dangling
profile tile, and deleting a person removes references to that identity.

fields.name: person
fields.doc: The person tagged in that post.

tables.name: carousel_slide
tables.doc: One slide of a carousel post, in slide order.

fields.name: post
fields.doc: The carousel post this slide belongs to.

fields.name: file
fields.doc: The slide image stored in Spock's byte plane.

tables.name: comment
tables.doc: A comment on a post. Thread order and age labels derive from `created_at`.

tables.name: like
tables.doc:
A like edge. The composite key makes a like at-most-once per (user, post)
by construction — no separate `unique` needed.

tables.name: save
tables.doc:
A post saved to one user's private library. The composite key makes saving
idempotent. Unlike a like, this edge is viewer-private state: it produces no
notification and has no public count.

fields.name: user
fields.doc: The user who saved the post. RPCs always stamp the current actor.

tables.name: storage_object
tables.doc: A stored file. Metadata for a byte object whose bytes live in the runtime's blob store: created pending on upload, committed when its bytes land, swept when abandoned or unreferenced (RFD 0018).

fns.name: nonempty
fns.doc:
A comment body must contain something other than spaces, tabs, or line
breaks (RFD 0013 validator; derives `comment_body_invalid` on the whole
floor, fn escapes included).
sql: SELECT length(trim(:s, ' ' || char(9, 10, 13))) > 0

fns.name: like_post
fns.doc:
Like a post as the current actor (`X-Spock-Actor`). Idempotent: liking a
post you already like returns the existing like row and does not touch
any counter because the displayed count is derived from like rows. Refuses
`not_authorized` when anonymous and answers `not_found` when the post does
not exist (the returning SELECT finds no row, so the transaction rolls back).
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

INSERT INTO "like" ("user", post)
    SELECT spock_actor(), p.id FROM post p WHERE p.id = :post
    ON CONFLICT ("user", post) DO NOTHING
SELECT * FROM "like" WHERE post = :post AND "user" = spock_actor()

fns.name: unlike_post
fns.doc:
Remove the actor's like. Idempotent: unliking a post you don't like (or
a post that doesn't exist) returns null and changes nothing.
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

DELETE FROM "like" WHERE post = :post AND "user" = spock_actor()
    RETURNING *

fns.name: save_post
fns.doc:
Save a post to the current actor's private library. Idempotent: saving an
already-saved post returns the existing edge without refreshing its time.
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

INSERT INTO save ("user", post)
    SELECT spock_actor(), p.id FROM post p WHERE p.id = :post
    ON CONFLICT ("user", post) DO NOTHING
SELECT * FROM save WHERE post = :post AND "user" = spock_actor()

fns.name: unsave_post
fns.doc:
Remove a post from the current actor's private library. Idempotent: an
absent save edge returns null and changes nothing.

params.name: post
params.doc: The post to remove from saved posts.

sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

DELETE FROM save WHERE post = :post AND "user" = spock_actor()
    RETURNING *

fns.name: add_comment
fns.doc:
Comment on a post as the current actor, returning the authority's echo of
the new row — including its minted UUIDv7 `id` and real `created_at`.
An empty or whitespace-only body trips the `comment_body_invalid` check; a
missing post is `not_found` (the answering INSERT..SELECT inserts nothing).
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

INSERT INTO comment (post, author, body)
    SELECT p.id, spock_actor(), :body
    FROM post p WHERE p.id = :post
    RETURNING *

fns.name: follow_user
fns.doc:
Follow another person. Idempotent; a repeated follow returns the existing
edge. Counts and lists update automatically because they derive from rows.
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL
SELECT spock_refuse('cannot_follow_self') WHERE spock_actor() = :target

INSERT INTO follow (follower, followed)
    SELECT spock_actor(), u.id FROM "user" u WHERE u.id = :target
    ON CONFLICT (follower, followed) DO NOTHING

SELECT * FROM follow
    WHERE follower = spock_actor() AND followed = :target

fns.name: unfollow_user
fns.doc: Stop following another person. Idempotent; an absent edge returns null.
sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

DELETE FROM follow
    WHERE follower = spock_actor() AND followed = :target
    RETURNING *

fns.name: mark_story_viewed
fns.doc:
Mark one story frame as viewed. Idempotent; the resulting ring state is
computed by comparing all story rows with the viewer's view edges.

params.name: story
params.doc: The story frame that became visible.

sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

INSERT INTO story_view (viewer, story)
    SELECT spock_actor(), s.id FROM story s WHERE s.id = :story
    ON CONFLICT (viewer, story) DO NOTHING

SELECT * FROM story_view
    WHERE viewer = spock_actor() AND story = :story

fns.name: create_image_post
fns.doc:
Publish one uploaded image as a new feed post. The caller first mints a
signed upload URL, PUTs the bytes, then passes the returned storage-object
id here. Only a committed JPEG, PNG, or WebP object owned by the current
actor may be attached; seed objects deliberately have no owner and cannot
be claimed. Storage v0 records the upload's Content-Type header; byte-level
image decoding/sniffing is deliberately beyond this local-dev PoC.
Retrying the same object is idempotent: the first published post is returned.

params.name: image
params.doc: A committed image object minted by the current actor.

params.name: caption
params.doc: The new post's caption; empty publishes without a caption.

params.name: alt
params.doc:
Accessible alternative text for the image; empty is accepted when the
author does not provide one.

sql: SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized') WHERE spock_actor() IS NULL

SELECT spock_refuse('image_not_ready')
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM storage_object
      WHERE id = :image AND state = 'committed'
    )

SELECT spock_refuse('not_authorized')
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM storage_object
      WHERE id = :image AND owner = spock_actor()
    )

SELECT spock_refuse('unsupported_media_type')
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM storage_object
      WHERE id = :image
        AND content_type IN ('image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp')
    )

INSERT INTO post (
      author, caption, media_kind, media_file, media_alt
    )
    SELECT spock_actor(), :caption, 'image', :image, :alt
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM post AS existing WHERE existing.media_file = :image
    )

SELECT * FROM post
    WHERE author = spock_actor() AND media_file = :image
    ORDER BY published_at
    LIMIT 1

fields.bio: Food and travel photographer in Lisbon. Usually awake before the trams.
Ceramics and slow mornings. Small-batch studio work from Portland.
Surf photographer, road-trip cook, and reluctant morning person.
Night skies and northern water, filmed around Tromsø.
Bread notebook, tiny kitchen, stubborn sourdough starter.
Istanbul by ferry. Architecture, tea, and ordinary light.
Natural-fiber clothes made in a very crowded studio.
Murals, community courts, and an unreliable jump shot.
Long climbs, quiet roads, and coffee at the turnaround.

fields.caption: Breakfast before the first tram
fields.media_alt: Pastéis de nata cooling on a marble counter

fields.caption: Then the city wakes
fields.media_alt: Tram rails catching the first light in Lisbon

fields.caption: Saturday palette
fields.media_alt: Crates of citrus stacked at the morning market

fields.caption: One pull, no edits
fields.media_alt: Lena throwing a tall clay cylinder

fields.caption: The unglamorous half of studio day
fields.media_alt: Rows of glaze buckets labelled by firing cone

fields.caption: Reset for tomorrow
fields.media_alt: Morning light crossing a clean ceramics workbench

fields.caption: Last night at camp
fields.media_alt: Campfire on a bluff above the break

fields.caption: It finally arrived
fields.media_alt: Long left-hand wave peeling along a desert point

fields.caption: Pack up before the wind
fields.media_alt: Surfboard fins silhouetted against a Baja sunrise

fields.caption: Still warm
fields.media_alt: Freshly sliced sourdough loaf

fields.caption: Fitting day
fields.media_alt: Linen garments arranged by shade

fields.caption: Worth the climb
fields.media_alt: Road bike at Copper Pass

fields.caption: New copper-red test tiles out of the kiln. Cone 10, heavy reduction — the speckle finally behaved.
fields.media_alt: Grid of copper-red glaze test tiles on a maple bench

fields.caption: Three days down the Baja coast. Swell arrived on the last morning, as it always does.
Aurora over the fjord last night — the whole sky was breathing.
fields.media_alt: Green aurora curtains over a dark fjord

fields.caption: Day 400 of the starter. She's earned a name: Clint Yeastwood.
fields.media_alt: Open crumb of a sourdough loaf, sliced on a flour-dusted board

fields.caption: Morning ferry across the Bosphorus. Tea, gulls, and nowhere to be until noon.
fields.media_alt: Ferry deck railing over blue water, city skyline behind

fields.caption: Studio lookbook, page one. Linen in every weight we could mill.
fields.media_alt: Folded linen garments stacked by shade on a workbench

fields.caption: Finished the mural at the 9th street court. Paint holds up better than my jumper.
fields.media_alt: Basketball court painted with bold geometric shapes

fields.caption: 120km of switchbacks and one very smug goat. Copper Pass, you were worth it.
fields.media_alt: Road bike leaning on a stone wall at a mountain pass

fields.caption: Copper glaze in close-up, before the wax cooled.
fields.media_alt: Copper-red glaze tiles

fields.caption: A quiet stack of bowls waiting for bisque firing.
fields.media_alt: Stack of unglazed bowls

fields.caption: Kiln Tetris, level thirty-seven.
fields.media_alt: Kiln shelf mid-load

fields.caption: Celadon tests after a slower cool-down.
fields.media_alt: Celadon test cups

fields.caption: Fresh reclaim, wedged and ready for tomorrow.
fields.media_alt: Wedging table with fresh clay

fields.caption: Dinner plates with just enough iron speckle.
fields.media_alt: Iron-speckled dinner plates

fields.caption: Pulling one tall cylinder before lunch.
fields.media_alt: Throwing a tall cylinder

fields.caption: Labelling day. Future me will be grateful.
fields.media_alt: Glaze buckets labelled by cone

fields.caption: Seven o'clock light across the clean bench.
fields.media_alt: Morning light across the studio bench

fields.caption: The batch that vanished before I finished the coffee.
fields.media_alt: Pastéis de nata on a marble counter

fields.caption: Rails holding the first light on Rua da Conceição.
fields.media_alt: Tram rails catching dawn light

fields.caption: Saturday citrus, arranged better than any still life.
fields.media_alt: Market citrus stacked in crates

fields.caption: Blue after blue after blue.
fields.media_alt: Tiled facade in alternating blues

fields.caption: Sardines, smoke, lemon. Nothing else needed.
fields.media_alt: Grilled sardines over coals

fields.caption: The last ferry left a gold line all the way home.
fields.media_alt: Ferry wake at golden hour

fields.alt: Long left-hand wave peeling along a desert point
Campfire on the bluff above the break at dusk
Board fins silhouetted against the sunrise

fields.body: That copper red is unreal. What cone are you firing to?
The third tile down — that speckle! Saving this for glaze inspiration.
Would buy the whole batch honestly. Seconds sale when?
These would look wild as a court-side mosaic. Collab?
That road looks nearly as good as the wave.
Frame two belongs on a wall.
Goat photo or it did not happen.
